Article.ArticleTitle: Impact of Being Underweight on the Safety and Efficacy of Atrial Fibrillation Ablation: An Analysis From the Japanese Catheter Ablation Registry.

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: Dr Inoue received honoraria from Daiichi Sankyo, Nippon Boehringer Ingelheim, Bristol Myers Squibb, Bayer Yakuhin, Johnson & Johnson K.K., Boston Scientific Japan, and Medtronic Japan. Dr Masuda received honoraria from Medtronic Japan, Johnson & Johnson K.K., Boston Scientific Japan, Abbott Medical Japan, and Nihon Kohden. Dr Satomi received honoraria from Medtronic Japan, Abbott Medical Japan, and Japan Lifeline. Dr Yamane received honoraria from Daiichi Sankyo, Medtronic Japan, and BEG Company Ltd. and research grants from Nippon Boehringer Ingelheim. Dr Kusano received honoraria from Daiichi Sankyo, Nippon Boehringer Ingelheim, Biotronik Japan, Bayer Yakuhin, Pfizer Japan, and Medtronic Japan and research grants from Medtronic Japan, Hitachi, Biotronic Japan, Mebix, and JSR. Dr Shimizu received honoraria from Daiichi Sankyo, Nippon Boehringer Ingelheim, Bristol Myers Squibb, Bayer Yakuhin, Pfizer Japan, Novartis Pharma, Johnson & Johnson K.K., Boston Scientific Japan, and Medtronic Japan and research grants from Daiichi Sankyo and Nippon Boehringer Ingelheim. Dr Miyamoto reports funding/grants received from Medtronic, Biosense Webster, Boston, and Abbott and speakers’ bureaus from Medtronic, Biosense Webster, Boston, and Abbott outside the submitted work and is affiliated with a department endowed by Medtronic outside the submitted work. The other authors report no conflicts.
